communicative_purpose: To refer to the traditional French style of cooking
trigger_situations: Food discussions, French culture, cooking traditions
variations: la gastronomie française, les plats français, la cuisine de France
common_mistakes: French cuisine varies by region; not one monolithic style
example_1: La cuisine française est connue dans le monde entier.
example_2: La cuisine française a des influences régionales variées.
example_3: On a découvert la cuisine française lors de notre voyage en Provence.
example_1_translation: French cuisine is known all over the world.
example_2_translation: French cuisine has varied regional influences.
example_3_translation: We discovered French cuisine during our trip to Provence.

communicative_purpose: To describe taking a midday break for lunch
trigger_situations: Work schedules, lunch time, meal routines
contexts: Work, lunch, French work culture, daily schedules
variations: prendre le déjeuner, aller manger, pause midi
common_mistakes: In France, lunch is typically 12-14h and taken seriously as a break
example_1: En France, on fait la pause déjeuner à 12h30.
example_2: La pause déjeuner dure généralement une heure en France.
example_3: Je prends mon déjeuner à la cantine avec les collègues.
example_1_translation: In France, we take a lunch break at 12:30 p.m.
example_2_translation: The lunch break generally lasts one hour in France.
example_3_translation: I have my lunch in the canteen with my colleagues.

communicative_purpose: To refer to markets selling local and regional products
contexts: Markets, local food, regional products, terroir
variations: le marché local, le marché paysan, le marché de producers
common_mistakes: 'Terroir' refers to the land/environment that gives food its character
example_1: Le marché du terroir propose des produits de notre région.
example_2: On achète au marché du terroir pour soutenir les producers locaux.
example_3: Le fromage du terroir a un goût authentique et unique.
example_1_translation: The local market offers products from our region.
example_2_translation: We buy from the local market to support local producers.
example_3_translation: Local cheese has an authentic and unique taste.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the daily menu specials
trigger_situations: Restaurant dining, daily menus, lunch specials
contexts: Restaurants, cafés, daily menus, French bistro culture
variations: le plat du jour, la formule du jour, le menu à Prix fixe
common_mistakes: Daily specials are often the freshest and best value options
example_1: Les plats du jour sont affichés sur l'ardoise à l'entrée.
example_2: Je prends toujours le plat du jour, c'est habituellement très bon.
example_3: Le chef change les plats du jour selon les arrivages du marché.
example_1_translation: The daily specials are displayed on the slate at the entrance.
example_2_translation: I always have the daily special, it's usually very good.
example_3_translation: The chef changes the daily specials according to market arrivals.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a drink taken before a meal
trigger_situations: Pre-dinner drinks, social gatherings, French customs
contexts: French dining culture, drinks, social customs
variations: l'apéro, le verre, le Kir, le porto
common_mistakes: 'Apéro' is casual slang; 'apéritif' is formal; both mean pre-dinner drink
example_1: On propose souvent un apéritif avant le dîner en France.
example_2: Voulez-vous un apéritif ? On a du Kir et du pastis.
example_3: L'apéro avec les amis commence à 19h avant le dîner.
example_1_translation: We often offer an aperitif before dinner in France.
example_2_translation: Would you like an aperitif? We have Kir and pastis.
example_3_translation: The aperitif with friends starts at 7 p.m. before dinner.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a selection of various cheeses
variations: le plateau de fromage, les fromages, la sélection de fromages
common_mistakes: French have hundreds of cheeses; 'crudités' means raw vegetables, not cheese
example_1: Le plateau de fromages include les grands classiques français.
example_2: Après le plat principal, on propose un plateau de fromages.
example_3: Un bon plateau de fromages doit avoir au moins 5 varieties.
example_1_translation: The cheese platter includes the great French classics.
example_2_translation: After the main course, we offer a cheese platter.
example_3_translation: A good cheese board should have at least 5 varieties.

communicative_purpose: To refer to the art of fine cooking and dining
trigger_situations: Fine dining, cooking culture, French traditions
contexts: French gastronomy, fine dining, culinary arts
variations: l'art culinaire, la haute cuisine, la cuisine raffinée
common_mistakes: UNESCO declared French gastronomy a world heritage in 2010
example_1: La Gastronomie française est inscrite au patrimoine mondial de l'UNESCO.
example_2: La Gastronomie demande du temps et de la patience.
example_3: Ce restaurant étoilé代表 la haute Gastronomie française.
example_1_translation: French Gastronomy is listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
example_3_translation: This Michelin-starred restaurant offers haute French gastronomy.

communicative_purpose: To refer to eating outdoors in nature
contexts: Parks, nature, outdoor dining, summer activities
variations: le picnic, le déjeuner dehors, manger en plein air
common_mistakes: 'Pique-nique' is French; English 'picnic' is borrowed back
example_1: On organise un pique-nique dans le parc ce weekend.
example_2: Le pique-niqueinclude du pain, du fromage et du vin.
example_3: Il fait beau pour un pique-nique au bord de la rivière.
example_1_translation: We're having a picnic in the park this weekend.
example_2_translation: The picnic includes bread, cheese and wine.
example_3_translation: The weather is nice for a picnic by the river.

communicative_purpose: To refer to an elaborate, multi-course meal
trigger_situations: Fine dining, special occasions, French cuisine
contexts: Gastronomy, fine dining, celebrations, French culture
variations: le festin, le dîner gastronomique, le menu dégustation
common_mistakes: A true gastronomic meal has multiple courses and takes hours
example_1: Un repas gastronomique peut durer 3 à 4 heures avec many plats.
example_2: Pour notre anniversaire, on a réservé un repas gastronomique.
example_3: Le repas gastronomique include toujours du vin paired avec chaque plat.
example_1_translation: A gourmet meal can last 3 to 4 hours with many dishes.
example_2_translation: For our anniversary, we booked a gourmet meal.
example_3_translation: The gourmet meal always includes wine paired with each dish.

communicative_purpose: To refer to traditional cooking methods and dishes
variations: la recette traditionnelle, le plat ancestral, l'héritage culinaire
common_mistakes: French regions have very different culinary traditions
example_1: La tradition culinairesurligne l'importance des produits frais.
example_2: Chaque région de France a sa propre tradition culinaire.
example_3: Les grands-mères transmettent la tradition culinaire aux petits-enfants.
example_1_translation: Culinary tradition highlights the importance of fresh produce.
example_2_translation: Each region of France has its own culinary tradition.
example_3_translation: Grandmothers pass on the culinary tradition to their grandchildren.

communicative_purpose: To indicate eating enough to be satisfied
variations: se rassasier, être rassasié, manger suffisamment
common_mistakes: 'Faim' means hunger; 'à sa faim' means to one's hunger/satisfaction
example_1: En France, on prend le temps de manger à sa faim.
example_2: Ce menú est copieux, j'ai mangé à ma faim facilement.
example_3: Après le festin, on a mangé à sa faim et on était rassasiés.
example_1_translation: In France, we take the time to eat our fill.
example_2_translation: This menu is plentiful, I ate my fill easily.
example_3_translation: After the feast, we ate our fill and were full.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a truck selling street food
trigger_situations: Street food, trendy dining, urban food
contexts: Street food, food trucks, urban dining, modern cuisine
variations: le camion de nourriture, le truck de street food, le food vendor
common_mistakes: English 'food truck' commonly used in French; 'camion-restaurant' is rare
example_1: Le food truck de tacos est installé sur la place du marché.
example_2: Les food trucks sont très tendance dans les grandes villes françaises.
example_3: On a mangé à un food truck pour essayer quelque chose de nouveau.
example_1_translation: The taco food truck is located on the market square.
example_2_translation: Food trucks are very trendy in major French cities.
example_3_translation: We ate at a food truck to try something new.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a late morning meal combining breakfast and lunch
trigger_situations: Weekend meals, late mornings, social dining
contexts: Weekends, social dining, modern food culture
variations: le petit-déjeuner tardif, le déjeuner-brunch, le breakfast
common_mistakes: English 'brunch' very common in French; French adaptation rare
example_1: Le brunch du dimanche est devenu une tradition pour nous.
example_2: On propose un brunch avec des huevos, des pancakes et du café.
example_3: Le brunch à la française inclut des croissants et du café au lait.
example_1_translation: Sunday brunch has become a tradition for us.
example_2_translation: We offer a brunch with huevos, pancakes and coffee.
example_3_translation: The French brunch includes croissants and café au lait.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a chef who has received Michelin stars
contexts: Michelin guide, fine dining, celebrity chefs
variations: le chef Michelin, le cuisinier étoilé, le maestro
common_mistakes: Michelin stars: 1 star (very good), 2 stars (excellent), 3 stars (exceptional)
collocates: trois étoiles, restaurant étoilé, célèbre, Bocuse
example_1: Paul Bocuse était un chef étoilé legendary de la cuisine française.
example_2: Ce restaurant a un chef étoilé qui cooks incredible dishes.
example_3: Les chefs étoilés créent des œuvres culinaires artistique.
example_1_translation: Paul Bocuse was a legendary star chef of French cuisine.
example_2_translation: This restaurant has a star chef who cooks incredible dishes.
example_3_translation: Star chefs create artistic culinary works.

communicative_purpose: To refer to a workplace or school cafeteria
trigger_situations: Work lunches, school meals, institutional dining
variations: le self, le réfectoire, la cafétéria, le self-service
common_mistakes: French school cantines are an important social institution
collocates: école, travail, repas, équilibré, cantine scolaire
example_1: Les enfants mangent à la cantine de l'école tous les jours.
example_2: La cantine de mon bureau propose un menu équilibrédié.
example_3: La cantine traditionnelle offre un repas complet avec entrée, plat, dessert.
example_1_translation: The children eat in the school canteen every day.
example_2_translation: My office canteen offers a balanced menu.
example_3_translation: The traditional canteen offers a complete meal with starter, main course, dessert.
